objective.content_objective: Students will solve multi-step equations with variables on both sides
objective.student_goal: I will solve equations by combining like terms and using inverse operations
objective.wida_objective: Students will use mathematical language to explain equation-solving steps (ELD Standard 3, Levels 3-5)

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Review solving one-step and two-step equations. Quick quiz on whiteboard with 3 problems.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Direct instruction on solving equations with variables on both sides. Model 3-4 examples on board, explaining each step. Emphasize combining like terms and inverse operations. Students practice 5 problems together as a class.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may forget to combine like terms before isolating the variable. Some may incorrectly subtract or add the same variable term from both sides.

assessment.primary_assessment: Exit ticket: Solve 2 multi-step equations. Check for understanding of the process and accuracy.

homework.original_content: Complete practice problems 1-15 on page 47. Show all work and check answers.

objective.content_objective: Students will apply equation-solving skills to word problems
objective.student_goal: I will translate word problems into equations and solve them
objective.wida_objective: Students will interpret and create mathematical word problems using academic vocabulary (ELD Standard 3, Levels 3-5)

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review homework problems. Address common errors. Quick pair-share: When do we use equations in real life?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Teach strategy for translating word problems into equations. Model 3 examples: age problems, money problems, distance problems. Small group work: Groups of 3-4 solve word problems together.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle to identify the variable or set up the equation correctly. Some may solve the equation but forget to answer the original question.

assessment.primary_assessment: Formative: Check group work and individual practice. Note students needing additional support.

homework.original_content: Create your own word problem that requires a multi-step equation. Solve it and explain your thinking.

objective.content_objective: Students will solve equations with fractions and decimals
objective.student_goal: I will clear fractions and decimals from equations before solving
objective.wida_objective: Students will explain fraction and decimal operations using precise mathematical language (ELD Standard 3, Levels 3-5)

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review multiplying to clear fractions. Quick practice: Multiply both sides of simple equations by the LCD.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Demonstrate clearing fractions by multiplying by LCD. Show clearing decimals by multiplying by powers of 10. Practice 6 problems as a class, then independent work.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may forget to multiply ALL terms by the LCD. Some may incorrectly simplify fractions after multiplying.

assessment.primary_assessment: Quiz: 5 equations with fractions and decimals. Assess both process and accuracy.

homework.original_content: Complete worksheet: 10 problems mixing fractions and decimals. Show all steps.

objective.content_objective: Students will solve equations with no solution or infinite solutions
objective.student_goal: I will identify when equations have no solution or infinite solutions
objective.wida_objective: Students will justify mathematical conclusions using evidence and reasoning (ELD Standard 3, Levels 4-6)

anticipatory_set.original_content: Solve 2x + 3 = 2x + 5 as a class. Discuss what happens and why.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Explain special cases: no solution (contradiction) and infinite solutions (identity). Model 4 examples. Students work in pairs to identify and explain special cases.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may think they made an error when they get 0 = 5 or 3 = 3. They may not understand what these results mean.

assessment.primary_assessment: Partner work: Solve and classify 8 equations. Explain reasoning for each classification.

homework.original_content: Practice problems: Solve and classify 12 equations. Write explanations for special cases.

objective.content_objective: Students will demonstrate mastery of multi-step equation solving
objective.student_goal: I will solve various types of equations and explain my process
objective.wida_objective: Students will communicate mathematical thinking orally and in writing (ELD Standard 3, Levels 3-6)

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review key concepts from the week. Students share one thing they learned and one question they have.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Review all equation types covered this week. Students rotate through 4 stations: basic equations, word problems, fractions/decimals, special cases. Teacher circulates to provide support.

misconceptions.original_content: Review common errors from the week. Address any persistent misconceptions through targeted mini-lessons.

assessment.primary_assessment: Unit test: 20 questions covering all equation types. Mix of computation and word problems.

homework.original_content: Study for unit test. Review notes and completed assignments. Complete any missing work.
